summary: GM subsystem decomp-matching slice split from #2617. The loaded diff changes seven files under src/melee/gm, mostly small source-shape improvements, local declaration/order tweaks, field access refinements, JObj helper usage corrections, and loop/index rewrites. Bot reporting for this PR slice cites 2 new matches and 11 unmatched-item improvements, including gm_80164330 and fn_80185E34 reaching 100%. One review comment flags that a constant should be retained instead of using a data symbol/value replacement in gm_1832.c.

categories: local variable and loop shape tuning

key_files.role: Matched/improved GM functions by simplifying stage-mask checks, ternary bool return, and replacing an inline unlock-character search with fn_801605EC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists gm_80164330 as a new 100% match and gm_80164910 as improved

key_files.role: Small declaration-order change around gm_8016EDDC local u8 variables with PAD_STACK still present.
Improved matching by inlining/removing temporary flag/pointer locals, converting duplicated score/ranking code into indexed loops, and adding a cast on an array index.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_801701C0 and gm_80173EEC as improved

key_files.role: Recovered fields in gm_1832_8047368C_t, separated translation and scale Vec3 temporaries, changed demo-player data source to lbl_8047368C, and received review feedback about keeping a constant.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1832_8047368C_t fields, fn_8018325C scale Vec3, fn_80185E34 hunks
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_80185E34 as a new 100% match
review_comments.md: PsiLupan comment on sticking with the constant

key_files.role: Multiple tournament/menu GM matching improvements: temporary sign/control changes, declaration order changes, loop initialization shape, JObj scale setter corrections, direct hsd_obj access, condition flip, and pointer-increment loop for bounce_y.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018E46C, fn_8018E85C, gm_801905F0, fn_80198824, fn_80198EBC hunks
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists five gm_18A5 functions as improved

key_files.role: Removed a temporary float by passing expressions directly to HSD_JObj translation setters and changed loop counter declaration type/order.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists gm_801A9630 and gm_801A85E4 as improved

key_files.role: Declaration order and separate loop-index variable adjustment in gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter audio-mask construction.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter as improved

what_changed: The PR body says this is a directory/subsystem split from #2617 scoped to src/melee/gm so matching improvements are easier to review.
The loaded changed-file metadata shows seven modified files: gm_1601.c, gm_16AE.c, gm_16F1.c, gm_1832.c, gm_18A5.c, gm_1A4C.c, and gmtou.c.
decomp-dev reported +552 matched-code bytes, 2 new matches, and 11 improvements in unmatched items for this PR slice.

gm_1601.c simplified gm_80164330 by removing a temporary pointer, directly testing gmMainLib_8015CC58()->stage_mask, and using a ternary return; gm_80164910 now calls fn_801605EC instead of an inline goto-based lookup loop.
gm_16F1.c converted a hand-special-cased score/ranking sequence in fn_801701C0 into indexed loops over six entries, removed pointer-walk locals, and kept the score/ranking expressions indexed.

gm_1832.c expanded a previously padded struct into xEC/xED/xEE/xEF and related small arrays, separated position and scale Vec3 locals, and changed fn_80185E34 to read player setup arrays from lbl_8047368C.

gm_18A5.c includes several small match-shape changes: local declaration order changes, explicit temporary sign handling, for-loop initialization changes, TranslateX/Y calls changed to ScaleX/Y in fn_8018E85C, direct gobj->hsd_obj assignment, an inverted lbl_804D667C condition, and pointer increment over bounce_y.
gm_1A4C.c removed a temporary float used only for HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Z arguments and changed a loop index declaration from s32 to int.
gmtou.c introduced a separate j loop variable for a four-player audio-mask loop.

smart_moves.title: Split a broad matching branch by subsystem/path
smart_moves.details: The PR body says the work was split from #2617 by directory/subsystem and scoped to src/melee/gm, reducing review surface for GM-only matching changes.

smart_moves.title: Used bot report to identify narrow match wins
smart_moves.details: The PR includes decomp-dev reporting that identifies exact units and functions improved, including two new 100% matches.

smart_moves.title: Replaced hand-expanded repeated ranking code with natural indexed loops
smart_moves.details: fn_801701C0 moved from a special case for x58[0] plus pointer loops over entries 1..5 to indexed loops over k, idx, and j from 0..5.

smart_moves.title: Separated position and scale temporaries for JObj operations
smart_moves.details: fn_8018325C now uses Vec3 pos for translation and Vec3 scale for HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetScale instead of reusing pos for both concepts.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Natural indexed loops can replace generated-looking special cases when repeated blocks differ only by array index.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801701C0 changed from x58[0] special-case plus pointer walks to for loops over k, idx, and j

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use separate locals when a vector has distinct semantic roles, such as translation versus scale, even if the type is the same.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added Vec3 scale and changed HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetScale to use &scale

decomp_lessons.lesson: Reviewer feedback in this PR supports keeping ordinary constants rather than replacing them with a symbol solely for matching.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: 'should stick with using the constant here'
review_comments.md: hunk shows Player_SetFacingDirection changed from 0.0f toward lbl_804DA540 in the commented version

decomp_lessons.lesson: Direct helper calls can replace local hand-written searches when the helper's purpose is already available in the TU.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inline NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS search and found_char goto replaced with fn_801605EC(internal_id)

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Temporary locals named for local role or API concept
naming_conventions.details: The PR adds names such as scale and bounce_y where the local role is clear, replacing reuse of pos or direct indexed access in the edited hunks.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Address-style and offset-style names remain when semantics are not recovered
naming_conventions.details: The PR keeps names such as lbl_8047368C, xEF, xF1, xF7, and xFD for newly exposed struct fields rather than inventing semantic names.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Use conventional short loop counters when matching shape benefits
naming_conventions.details: The PR uses i, j, k, idx, inner_idx, and outer_idx across loop rewrites rather than descriptive but unsupported names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration order and local lifetime tuning
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Several hunks reorder declarations or initialize locals at declaration/loop sites, likely to affect MWCC stack/register shape; evidence is diff and bot score only, not explicit objdiff notes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1A4C.c gm_801A9630 int i declaration

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_801701C0 was rewritten into indexed loops, fn_8018E85C moved inner_idx initialization outside the for header, fn_80198EBC increments a bounce_y pointer in the for header, and gmtou uses a separate j loop variable.
The diff removes temp_ret in gm_80164330, flags in fn_8016FFD4, and x in gm_801A85E4, replacing them with direct field/helper/expression use.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: API call correction for object transforms
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_8018E85C changes HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y to HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y for final_pos, and fn_80198824 uses gobj->hsd_obj directly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_18A5.c fn_8018E85C and fn_80198824 hunks

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK retained in some touched functions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: PAD_STACK remains in gm_8016EDDC, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A9630, gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter, and fn_80198EBC hunks; the PR evidence does not include a per-function justification beyond score improvements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK occurrences in touched hunks

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: As noted on another PR, should stick with using the constant here.
review_feedback.interpretation: Do not replace an ordinary constant with a symbol/value alias without evidence; the loaded review hunk shows the comment near Player_SetFacingDirection changing from 0.0f to lbl_804DA540 in the commented version.

follow_up_queries: fn_801701C0 natural loop x58 scores rankings
split from PR 2617 src/melee/gm matching improvements

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
human_pr_text.md: scope, split rationale, preliminary/local checks
raw_diff: full loaded diff for seven files
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report and review comment
notes: The loaded diff and changed_files metadata cover seven files, while the PR body's file list includes gmregclear.c, gmresult.c, and gmresultplayer.c; no loaded changed-file metadata or diff hunks for those three files support treating them as changed in this slice.

The PR body's preliminary report is for the full unsplit source branch and explicitly says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative for this draft; this intake therefore treats the decomp-dev PR comment as the slice-specific score evidence.
No local build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs are loaded beyond decomp-dev reporting and the PR body's git diff --check statement.

The review comment is clear that a constant should be retained, but the loaded final raw_diff shows 0.0f and 1.0f in the displayed fn_80185E34 hunk; this intake does not infer whether the comment caused a later revision.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural indexed loops can replace generated special-case plus pointer-walk code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2621, fn_801701C0 in gm_16F1.c improved by replacing a special case for x58[0] plus pointer walks over entries 1..5 with indexed loops over six entries for score and ranking computation.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_801701C0 improved

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep constants inline when review flags symbol replacement as inappropriate
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2621 review feedback on gm_1832.c says to stick with the constant in a hunk where Player_SetFacingDirection was changed away from 0.0f in the commented version, supporting caution against replacing ordinary constants with symbols without evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use distinct Vec3 locals for distinct JObj transform roles
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2621 changed fn_8018325C to use a separate Vec3 scale for HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetScale while keeping Vec3 pos for translation.

source_update_candidates.title: Reviewer constant feedback example for literals-and-data-ownership
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2621 as an example under literals-and-data-ownership/no-string-literal-symbol-regression adjacent guidance: reviewer feedback said to stick with a constant rather than replacing it with a symbol/value in gm_1832.c.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: 'should stick with using the constant here'
source_update_candidates.reason: The feedback aligns with existing standards, but promotion as a standard example should be curator-owned.

source_update_candidates.title: GM path slice for PR #2621
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2621 was scoped to src/melee/gm and changed seven loaded GM files in this slice: gm_1601.c, gm_16AE.c, gm_16F1.c, gm_1832.c, gm_18A5.c, gm_1A4C.c, and gmtou.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could be useful as a historical path fact, but the PR body lists additional files not present in loaded changed-file metadata, so curator should decide wording.

rejection_notes: Do not record gmregclear.c, gmresult.c, or gmresultplayer.c as changed files for this intake because they appear only in the PR body's file list and not in loaded changed_files metadata or raw diff.
Do not claim all PAD_STACK uses are justified by objdiff; the evidence only shows PAD_STACK remained in touched functions and that decomp-dev reported score improvements.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit constant comment.
